div.p: Building buckets with equal row counts, adapting to data distribution
Dense regions (low values) get narrow buckets for precise cost estimation. Sparse regions (outliers) get wide buckets but maintain row balance.

Equi-width (alternative) would create 4 equal ranges of 112.5 units. This would result in skewed bucket row counts (e.g., 14, 2, 1, 3), making selectivity estimation highly inaccurate for the dense region.

g.text: bucket_size = n // bucket_count # 20 // 4 = 5 rows per bucket
